message:
feat(stream): add FlatMap and Window combinators with bug fixes in chunks and flatten

Add two new stream combinators to the async stream module:

FlatMap combinator (asx_stream_flat_map_init):
- Maps each item from an outer stream to an inner sub-stream via a
  user-supplied flat_map_fn callback, then flattens all sub-streams
  into a single output stream.
- Gracefully handles NULL returns from the mapping function by skipping
  to the next outer item, allowing selective expansion.
- Tracks outer-done and current-sub-stream state to correctly drain
  each sub-stream before advancing the outer iterator.

Window combinator (asx_stream_window_init):
- Emits sliding windows of a configurable size over an input stream.
- Uses a circular buffer (capped at ASX_STREAM_WINDOW_MAX, default 16)
  to hold the current window state, yielding an asx_stream_window
  pointer on each poll.
- First yield occurs only after window_size items are buffered; if the
  inner stream is shorter than window_size, a single partial window is
  emitted on stream exhaustion.
- Clamps window_size to [1, ASX_STREAM_WINDOW_MAX] during init.

Bug fixes in existing combinators:
- chunks_poll: removed a redundant bounds check that was already
  guaranteed by the chunk-size break condition, simplifying the
  item-append path.
- flatten_poll: added a NULL-pointer guard on inner_ptr returned from
  the outer stream, skipping NULL inner streams instead of
  dereferencing them.

Documentation improvement:
- Expanded the dedup combinator's doc comment to clarify the pointer-
  validity requirement on inner-stream items across consecutive
  poll_next calls.

Tests:
- flat_map_basic: verifies mapping [1,2,3] where 1→[10,20], 2→[30],
  3→NULL(skip) produces [10,20,30].
- window_basic: verifies sliding window of size 3 over [1,2,3,4,5]
  yields three windows with correct circular-buffer state.
- window_short_stream: verifies that a stream shorter than window_size
  yields a single partial window with all available items.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
